Denver · Colorado

Denver
Movement
School

Strength, coordination, rhythm, and physical intelligence through shared practice.

See the Classes → Join WhatsApp Group →
Lakewood · Denver · Outdoors
Scroll
In your first class, you will fail at something. That is not a problem. It is the doorway.
To develop coordination you have to feel uncoordinated. To dance you have to step all over yourself, get tangled, lose the rhythm. To learn a skill you have to map the edges of your ability and give the brain time and sleep to create new pathways.
The practice
Failure
is part
of the map.

You come to class to get stronger, more coordinated, more mobile, more expressive, more capable. Then the work deepens. You start to notice how you meet difficulty, how you learn, how you recover, how you relate to other people — and how much of practice is simply the willingness to return.

Most people here are wanting to create something. A life, a feeling, a purpose, a connection, a depth. This is a practice ground of agency, of perception and action.

I trained at ApeCo Denver for years before I began teaching there. When the school closed, I kept organizing classes — outside first, sprinting and breath work, then eight-week curriculums of strength, locomotion, acrobatics, and balance. The people who showed up kept showing up. When the school closed, the practice didn't. Denver Movement School is what grew in the space that was left.

I have been a therapeutic bodyworker for seven years and a movement student for much longer. My work is shaped by manual therapy, nervous system practice, athletics, dance, fighting, acrobatics, and Human Movement Studies. My own education is active, not finished. I am always a student first.

Coordination begins with disorientation. Strength comes through stress and recovery. Skill grows through repeated contact with the edge of your current ability.

All levels are welcome. Nothing is forced. Everything can be scaled.
What we practice — Movimentica
01
Fighting
Striking, grappling, contact, timing, and the honest conversation between two bodies.
02
Dance
Breaking, house, hip-hop, improvisation, rhythm, and expressive movement.
03
Athletics
Running, jumping, climbing, balancing, rolling, and adapting to the environment.
04
Preparedness
Strength, mobility, breath, sprinting, conditioning, and usable capacity.
05
Acrobatics
Inversions, low flight, partner work, falling, support, and learning at the edge.
06
Inner Work
Attention, patience, fear, failure, feedback, and the ability to keep returning.
Join a class
Tuesday Evening · Weekly
General
Movement
The main weekly class. Strength, coordination, partner practice, rhythm, problem-solving, and physical challenge. You do not need to be advanced. You do need to be willing to try, fail, adjust, and keep practicing.
All levels Denver Ongoing
Reserve a Spot →
Monday Morning · Weekly
Sprint &
Athletics
Speed, power, elastic strength, conditioning, and the athletic foundations of movement practice. Small group training built around sprinting, breathing, recovery, and repeated exposure.
Early Morning Outdoors Ongoing
See the Tracks →
CYCLE
Enrolling for Fall
Full
Practice
Cycle
A structured training block across all six pillars — strength, rhythm, athletics, partnering, acrobatics, and inner work. Four sessions a week, eight weeks, a small cohort of people serious about developing a real practice. This is the school in full form.

Coming This Summer

Movement
Day
A free community gathering for people who want to move, meet the school, and feel the practice directly. Multiple teachers, multiple disciplines, one afternoon of shared training. Open to all levels. Date to be announced.

Come find us
Start
where
you are.
Everything can be adjusted to your level. You can stop, step back, observe, or try again. You will not be forced. You will be encouraged to pay attention, practice sincerely, and keep returning.

— Misha Lantsov
Founder, Denver Movement School
